# AIFF/FLAC decode fixture manifest

Every file here is synthetic, generated by `generate.sh` in this directory —
two sine tones (440 Hz, 660 Hz for the second channel) through `ffmpeg` and
the reference `flac` encoder. No external corpora, no licensing constraints
beyond the project license (MIT OR Apache-2.0).

## AIFF matrix — `aiff_{mono,stereo}_{16,24}_{44100,48000}.{aiff,wav}`

All eight channel/bit-depth/sample-rate combinations. Each `.wav` twin holds
the exact PCM samples repacked big-endian into the paired `.aiff` file, so
decoding both must produce identical planar `f32` buffers.

## FLAC — `flac_level{0,5,8}.flac`, `flac_blocksize.flac`, `flac_base16.wav`

16-bit stereo, 44.1 kHz. `flac_level0/5/8.flac` encode the same PCM at
compression levels 0, 5, and 8; `flac_blocksize.flac` encodes it at a
1152-sample block size against the encoder's 4096-sample default. All four
must decode back to `flac_base16.wav` exactly — FLAC is lossless.

## FLAC — `flac_24bit.flac`, `flac_base24.wav`

24-bit mono, 48 kHz, compression level 6. Exercises the wider bit depth
FLAC's stereo fixtures above don't cover.
